# pGOLD

> Decentralized gold exposure backed by pETH. How pGOLD compares to XAUT and PAXG, the exposure you actually get, and how to express any gold view.

Canonical URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/pgold
Markdown URL: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/minting/pgold.md
Section: Understand Polaris

Documentation index: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/llms.txt
Full documentation bundle: https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/llms-full.txt

---

pGOLD is a **pAsset that tracks the price of one ounce of gold**, issued against pETH collateral rather than bullion in a vault. It is decentralized gold exposure with no KYC, no size minimums, and no counterparty other than the Polaris protocol itself - no vault in Singapore, just a price feed and pETH collateral.

> **Note:** pGOLD is Testnet-active on Sepolia. See [Launch Status](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/launch-status) for the current list of live pAssets.

## pGOLD vs centralized gold (XAUT/PAXG)

pGOLD and the incumbent gold tokens both give gold-price exposure, but they are structurally different products.

Property | pGOLD | XAUT / PAXG

Collateral | pETH, fully onchain | Physical gold in custody
Redemption | Permissionless, no protocol-level minimum published yet | Issuer-gated; account requirements and redemption minimums depend on the issuer's current terms
KYC | None | Required to redeem
Physical custody | None - nothing to store or insure | Vaulted bullion, with custody risk
Counterparty | Polaris protocol only | Tether / Paxos as issuer

Image: pGOLD versus centralized gold tokens (XAUT/PAXG): pGOLD is backed by onchain pETH with permissionless, KYC-free access and Polaris as the only counterparty, while XAUT/PAXG are backed by vaulted bullion with issuer-gated, KYC-required redemption and custody risk

Under current issuer terms, the two centralized tokens together hold roughly \$5B in market cap, and redemption is gated behind KYC plus fees and minimums - XAUT, for example, charges 0.25% and requires roughly one gold bar (430 XAUT, about \$2.1M) to redeem. pGOLD inherits ETH-collateral and smart-contract risk where XAUT/PAXG inherit custodial and issuer risk. What pGOLD removes is the permissioned issuer layer.

pGOLD's collateral pricing composes two oracle feeds with the bonding curve: pETH is valued in gold terms as ETH/USD × the curve's pETH price ÷ gold/USD. A failure of either feed affects pGOLD pricing; see [Oracle risk](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/resources/risk-disclosure#oracle-risk).

## Borrowing pGOLD is not shorting gold

Image: Borrowing pGOLD is not shorting gold: deposit pETH and borrow pGOLD, then your strategy sets the exposure - hold or LP the gold pair for gold-neutral exposure, or sell pGOLD for pETH (or loop) to take short-gold exposure

Issuing pGOLD against pETH is a borrowing action; whether you end up long pETH, gold-neutral, or short gold depends on what you do with the pGOLD. Borrow 1 pGOLD and hold it and your gold exposure is neutral - you owe 1 pGOLD and hold 1 pGOLD. The position only becomes a short if you sell that pGOLD for a non-gold asset.

So almost any view on gold can be expressed with pGOLD:

Your view | How to express it

**Long gold** | Buy pGOLD and LP a gold-correlated pair
**Short gold, long ETH** | Post pETH, borrow pGOLD, then sell it or loop the position
**Long gold, short ETH** | Buy pGOLD and deposit it in the [Earn Vault](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield)
**Gold-neutral, long volume** | Borrow pGOLD and LP gold-correlated pairs

## Why borrow pGOLD

Polaris is designed to make borrowing attractive: borrowers pay interest but also receive pETH incentives on their position, so the *effective* rate can run low or even negative when incentives exceed interest. Because gold sees strong holding demand but comparatively little borrow demand, pGOLD is a natural fit for that flexibility. See [Yield Sources](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/yield/yield-sources) and [POLAR Tokenomics](https://tokenbrice.github.io/polaris-docs/polar/tokenomics) for the mechanics.
